name: Old National Bank
description_short: Old National Bank is a FDIC-insured regional bank offering checking, savings, CDs, mortgages, and investment services to personal and business customers.
description_long: Old National Bank is an FDIC-insured financial institution that operates as a regional bank serving personal and business customers. The bank's website indicates it operates First Midwest Bank as a division, suggesting it has undergone consolidation or rebranding in recent years. The institution maintains a traditional banking model with physical banking centers alongside digital services. The bank offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including personal checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), money market accounts, mortgages for purchase and refinance, home equity lines of credit (HELOCs), investment options, and wealth management services. For business customers, Old National provides commercial banking services including online banking platforms (ONPointe Treasury and ONPointe Essentials), commercial remote deposit, lockbox services, and business credit cards. The website prominently features current promotional rates, including Money Market accounts at 3.25% APY and 4-month CDs at 3.75% APY. Old National distinguishes itself through regional presence with multiple banking centers and surcharge-free ATM access, FDIC insurance backing, and a stated commitment to community engagement (notably referencing Louisville community involvement). The bank offers appointment scheduling, phone support at 1-800-731-2265, and multiple digital banking platforms for personal and business users. Current promotional offerings include up to $600 cash incentives for new checking customers and discounted HELOC introductory rates. As a regional bank, Old National Bank is best suited for customers seeking traditional banking services with local branch access and relationship banking. The main limitation is geographic availability, as it operates primarily as a regional institution rather than a national bank, and specific service details regarding minimum account balances, fees, and full product terms are not fully disclosed on the homepage.
services: Personal checking accounts with promotional incentives
Personal savings accounts and money market accounts
Certificates of Deposit (CDs) with competitive rates
Mortgage lending for home purchase and refinance
Home Equity Lines of Credit (HELOCs) with introductory rate discounts
Investment advisory and wealth management services
Business online banking platforms (ONPointe Treasury and ONPointe Essentials)
Commercial remote deposit and lockbox services
Business and personal credit card services
Digital banking enrollment and online account management
Mortgage rate quotes and lending consultations
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by U.S. government guarantee
Competitive promotional rates: 3.75% APY on 4-month CDs and 3.25% APY on Money Market accounts
Up to $600 cash bonus for new checking account customers completing qualifying activities
Multiple digital banking options including online banking, mobile banking, and specialized business platforms
Physical banking centers with surcharge-free ATM access across network
Comprehensive financial services including mortgages, wealth management, and investment options
Dedicated fraud prevention measures and customer service with explicit anti-phishing policies
cons: Limited to regional availability rather than national banking coverage
Minimum deposit requirements not clearly disclosed (Money Market requires $20,000 minimum; CD minimum $500)
Website does not provide transparent fee structures for checking, savings, or other products
Mortgage and loan products subject to credit review with no guaranteed approval
Limited information about business loan products and terms on main website
best_for: Regional customers seeking traditional banking with local branch relationships
Savers looking for competitive CD and money market rates with FDIC protection
Homebuyers and refinancers in Old National's service area
Small to mid-sized businesses needing integrated banking and treasury management services
diagnosis: Old National Bank is best for regional customers and businesses seeking traditional banking services with competitive rates, local branch access, and FDIC-insured deposits. The primary caveat is that the bank operates primarily as a regional institution, so service availability and specific product terms depend on geographic location and account type, with many details requiring direct contact or branch visit to confirm.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
